bounty_title: Best 60s POIDH ad from Ep 19 w/ Kenny
judging_note: Claude auto-checked X URL presence + @bettercallzaal tag + mentions via syndication endpoint. Duration (45-60s) and edited-vs-raw require manual playback - marked UNKNOWN where I cannot verify. Zaal confirms final verdict.

floor_rules.label: Edited (cuts, not a raw screen recording)

rubric_tiers.label: Craft
rubric_tiers.examples: Tight cuts, captions, hook in first 3s, clear CTA, watchable on mute

rubric_tiers.label: Substance
rubric_tiers.examples: Hits the POIDH thesis from Ep 19: permissionless real-world coordination, not generic crypto hype

rubric_tiers.label: Bonus
rubric_tiers.examples: Adds creative spin, attributes the source episode by name, encourages new bounties

submissions.x_text: How do we pull money to get things done in the real world? On BCZ YapZ Ep 19, @kennyistyping explains how POIDH brings transparent, permissionless coordination to life

rubric_score.distribution: STRONG - tagged all three: @bettercallzaal, @kennyistyping, @poidhxyz. Links poidh.xyz.
rubric_score.craft: Clean copy, emoji hook, two-question setup landing the POIDH answer. Needs playback to confirm edit quality.
rubric_score.substance: Directly cites Ep 19 + Kenny by name. Hits the permissionless coordination thesis exactly.
rubric_score.bonus: Cross-tagged POIDH team itself + linked poidh.xyz.

why_pick: Only submission that names BCZ YapZ Ep 19 + @kennyistyping in copy - reads as a real attribution, not a generic ad
Full tag-set: @bettercallzaal + @kennyistyping + @poidhxyz, plus poidh.xyz link
9 likes = second highest engagement of cohort
Two-question copy framing ('how do we...?' -> 'on Ep 19, X explains...') is classic ad structure
Lands the permissionless coordination thesis exactly as Kenny framed it in the episode
why_skip: Posted 32 min before deadline - little time to gather engagement, currently 0 replies
Video resolution is 480x270 (16:9, desktop crop) - less mobile-native than vertical formats
If duration is over 60s or under 45s, floor fails regardless of copy quality
key_unknowns: Duration: cannot auto-verify - watch to confirm 45-60s
Edit quality: thumbnail looks like a presentation slide; need to confirm tight cuts vs slide-show pacing
Does the audio hold up at 1x speed without subs?
submissions.if_video_lands: Winner-grade. Copy + tags + thesis fit are unmatched. Vote this one.
submissions.if_video_doesnt: Drops to a Top 4 finalist; lean toward 6608 (Dee) or 6616 (Ebuka) instead.
submissions.claude_notes: Strongest tag-set of the cohort. Posted right at deadline (23:27 PT on 2026-05-22). 9 likes is mid-pack. Floor PASS pending duration confirmation.

submissions.x_text: Coordinate funds for laser-specific real-world tasks without relying on a single middleman. POIDH changes the game, permissionless bounty structures into action. All you need is a wallet. Post your bounty now at poidh.xyz

rubric_score.distribution: STRONG - all three tags + link to poidh.xyz. Also cross-posted on Farcaster per POIDH submission text.
rubric_score.craft: Clear CTA, action-oriented copy. Hash-tag-free which reads cleaner.
rubric_score.bonus: Includes 'post your bounty now' CTA that pushes adoption.

why_pick: Most action-led CTA of the cohort - 'Post your bounty now at poidh.xyz' converts viewers into users
Full tag-set + poidh.xyz link in the body, not just mentions
POIDH submission description shows cross-post to Farcaster - real distribution effort
Copy reads like ad copy, not commentary - tight, no fluff
'All you need is a wallet' is a memorable one-liner that captures the permissionless aspect
why_skip: Only 2 likes despite strong copy - either posted to small audience or video isn't pulling viewers in
'POIDH changes the game' is generic crypto phrasing - thesis-fit weaker than 6645's Ep 19 framing
No mention of BCZ YapZ Ep 19 by name - feels like a general POIDH ad, not THIS episode's ad
Submitted right after 6645 - both at deadline, but this one has 4.5x less engagement
key_unknowns: Duration: amplify_video format suggests a polished upload, but length still unconfirmed
Does the video reference Kenny / Ep 19 / BCZ at all, or is it a clip-free 'about POIDH' explainer?
Why low engagement? Account reach or video weak?
submissions.if_video_lands: Winner-grade for ADOPTION goals - if you want a clip that converts strangers, this CTA structure is the model.
submissions.if_video_doesnt: Drops out of top 4 - copy alone doesn't carry without Ep 19 substance.

rubric_score.distribution: FAIL - no X URL submitted. POIDH description is plain text only, no link to a video post.
rubric_score.substance: Description text is generic POIDH boilerplate, not Ep 19 specific.

why_pick: n/a - no winning case. Floor fail by submission rules.
why_skip: Bounty rule 3 explicitly says: 'Submit the X post URL on POIDH at this bounty page.' This submission has only text, no URL
No video to evaluate at all - cannot score Craft, Distribution, or Substance
Description text is generic 'POIDH is a movement for internet trust' - not specific to Ep 19 or Kenny
Same wallet has an UNACCEPTED Round 1 claim 6368 - pattern of submitting incomplete claims
key_unknowns: Did Mariano post a video on X and forget to link it? Worth asking before final rejection
Is the IPFS image a screenshot of an X post? If yes, hunt for the URL
submissions.if_video_doesnt: Confirmed floor fail. Send rejection cast: 'Round 2 needed an X post URL. Resubmit on next bounty with the link.'
submissions.claude_notes: Bounty rule 3 explicitly says 'Submit the X post URL on POIDH at this bounty page.' Submission description has only text praising POIDH - no X URL. Floor FAIL on multiple rules. Note: this is the same wallet sitting on unaccepted Round 1 claim 6368.

submissions.x_text: From struggling with healthcare coordination to realizing anyone can pool money for real-world tasks like cleaning up trash in Seattle - permissionless, with proof required. Pics Or It Didn't Happen.

rubric_score.distribution: STRONG - all three tags. No external link added.
rubric_score.craft: Vivid concrete examples (healthcare coordination, trash cleanup in Seattle) rather than abstract. Bumper line 'Pics Or It Didn't Happen.' is memorable.
rubric_score.substance: Pulls a specific story arc from Ep 19. Concrete > generic.
rubric_score.bonus: Closes with the POIDH motto as a one-liner.

why_pick: Strongest substance write-up of the cohort - pulls two SPECIFIC examples Kenny mentioned (healthcare coordination + Seattle trash cleanup)
Closes with the POIDH motto as a punchline - free brand reinforcement
Full tag-set including @poidhxyz
Copy demonstrates the writer actually watched the episode - not a recycled crypto-Twitter pitch
Highest signal-per-word - 41 words but every one is doing work
amplify_video format = polished upload (not native X recorder)
why_skip: Only 2 likes despite strong copy - posted 4 days ago, no engagement traction
No CTA in the copy ('go submit your bounty' / 'try poidh.xyz') - leaves viewer with no next action
No external link to poidh.xyz in the body
Posted 2026-05-18, oldest of the strong candidates - has had a week and stayed at 2 likes
key_unknowns: Duration: amplify upload suggests intentional length, but 45-60s unconfirmed
Does the video reinforce the Seattle / healthcare examples or just say 'POIDH is cool'?
Is the video itself well-edited or did all the craft go into the tweet copy?
submissions.if_video_lands: TOP PICK. Copy proves comprehension of Ep 19; if video matches the copy quality, this is the winner.
submissions.if_video_doesnt: Still finishes Top 4 because copy is so strong - but pick 6608 or 6645 instead for distribution + tags.

submissions.x_text: this is POIDH. this is for POIDH. POIDH is one of the most active mini apps on Farcaster where you can join daily bounties or create your own for people to complete tasks, make content, and earn rewards.

rubric_score.distribution: STRONG - 4 tags including @farcaster_xyz. POIDH submission description says cross-posted across Farcaster + /poidh + /zao channels.
rubric_score.craft: Vertical 320x426 video = mobile-native, scroll-stopping format. Repetition hook ('this is POIDH / this is for POIDH') is ad-y.
rubric_score.substance: Frames POIDH as a Farcaster mini app for daily bounties - true but slightly off the Ep 19 thesis (real-world coordination > 'most active mini app').
rubric_score.bonus: Highest engagement of the cohort (11 likes) + cross-posted to Farcaster /zao + /poidh.

why_pick: Highest engagement of all 8 submissions (11 likes)
Only vertical (320x426 portrait) video - mobile-native, the only one that fits a phone screen without rotation
Most tags (4) including @farcaster_xyz - widest distribution surface
Cross-posted to Farcaster /poidh + /zao channels per POIDH submission description
Repetition hook ('this is POIDH. this is for POIDH.') is ad-pattern: announce-then-explain
Highest pick odds if 'best ad' means 'ad that DID best in the wild'
why_skip: Frames POIDH as 'most active mini app on Farcaster' - true, but NOT Kenny's real-world coordination thesis from Ep 19
No mention of BCZ YapZ Ep 19 or Kenny by name in the copy
Copy reads as a 'what is POIDH' explainer, not 'here is the moment from this episode'
Vertical format may not play well if Zaal wants to re-share on desktop surfaces
key_unknowns: Duration: vertical mobile format usually skews short (15-30s), might miss 45s floor
Does the video actually reference Ep 19, or is it a generic 'I made an ad for POIDH' clip?
Why the engagement spike vs other strong candidates - Dee's audience or video itself?
submissions.if_video_lands: TOP PICK if criteria favor 'ad that performs' over 'ad that quotes the episode'. Mobile-native + best engagement = best PR optics.
submissions.if_video_doesnt: Could drop if duration < 45s (vertical format risk). If video is generic, lean 6616 (Ebuka) for substance fit.

submissions.x_text: Crypto's hype is currently drowning out its actual help

rubric_score.distribution: MEDIUM - tagged @bettercallzaal + @kennyistyping but NOT @poidhxyz. One-line copy.
rubric_score.craft: Single-line copy. Strength = punchline if the video delivers. Weakness = no context for the scroll-by viewer.
rubric_score.substance: Strong thesis line ('crypto's hype is drowning out its actual help') but does not name POIDH in the copy.

why_pick: Strong opinion-driven hook line - 'crypto's hype is drowning out its actual help' - contrarian and stops scroll
Lets the video be the pitch - if the clip is great, copy stays out of its way
Posted earliest of the weak-floor group (2026-05-15) - had longest window for engagement
why_skip: Copy doesn't name POIDH AT ALL - viewer has no idea what the ad is for unless they watch
Missing @poidhxyz tag - poidh team won't see it, less reshare leverage
Only 2 likes after 10 days - hook didn't convert
Highest video-dependency risk in the cohort - if video is mediocre, the whole submission collapses
No CTA, no link, no thesis statement
key_unknowns: Does the video actually mention POIDH or BCZ YapZ at all?
Duration - cannot verify
Is the 'crypto hype' framing pro-POIDH or just contrarian filler?
submissions.if_video_lands: Surprise dark-horse pick - if the clip nails the 'real help' angle, the contrarian copy becomes a feature not a bug.
submissions.if_video_doesnt: Floor pass with no rubric points. Likely 5th or 6th place.

submissions.x_text: I think that permissionless aspect of anyone being able to put up a sufficient bounty...

rubric_score.craft: Cliffhanger copy (... ends mid-sentence). May intrigue or may frustrate scroll-by readers.
rubric_score.substance: On-thesis (permissionless bounty creation) but truncated - the video has to land the rest.

why_pick: On-thesis - 'permissionless aspect of anyone being able to put up a bounty' is exactly Kenny's Ep 19 message
Got 1 reply = only weak-floor submission with any conversation
amplify_video format suggests polished upload, not screen-record
Cliffhanger copy can work - creates curiosity if the video pays it off
why_skip: Missing @poidhxyz tag - distribution surface smaller
Cliffhanger copy is a coin-flip - works if video delivers, frustrates if it doesn't
2 likes + 1 reply after 11 days - low traction
Copy is incomplete on purpose - asks viewer to do work to find the payoff
Doesn't name BCZ YapZ Ep 19 or Kenny by name
key_unknowns: What's the rest of the sentence? Does the video complete the thought?
Duration - unconfirmed
Was the 1 reply positive or asking for clarification?
submissions.if_video_lands: Solid mid-pack pick - on-thesis content + amplify upload + some engagement.
submissions.if_video_doesnt: 5th-6th place. Copy alone doesn't earn a podium spot.

submissions.x_text: We're never gonna have good adoption if all crypto does is gambling.

rubric_score.craft: Punchy one-liner with anti-gambling angle. Stops the scroll.
rubric_score.substance: Captures the 'real utility' frame from Ep 19 without naming POIDH explicitly in copy - the video has to make the POIDH connection.

why_pick: Strong contrarian hook: 'crypto's just gambling' is the exact frame Kenny pushed back on in Ep 19
4 likes = third-highest engagement (only Dee at 11 and Monksage at 9 beat it)
Posted earliest of cohort (2026-05-14) - had 11 days to find audience
Quotable one-liner - the kind of copy that gets screenshotted
Punchline structure: copy sets up the problem, video presumably sets up POIDH as the answer
why_skip: Missing @poidhxyz tag
Doesn't name POIDH or BCZ YapZ Ep 19 in copy at all - relies 100% on video
If video doesn't make the POIDH bridge, the submission reads as generic anti-gambling, not a POIDH ad
0 replies despite 4 likes - viewers agreed but didn't engage further
key_unknowns: Does the video bridge from 'crypto = gambling problem' to 'POIDH = real-world coordination answer'?
Duration - unconfirmed
Is the 4 likes the account's normal baseline or an outlier?
submissions.if_video_lands: Dark-horse Top 3. Punchy copy + decent engagement + if the video lands the POIDH bridge.
submissions.if_video_doesnt: Reads as generic crypto-critique not POIDH ad. 4th-5th place.

pick_decision_matrix.if_optimizing_for_substance: 6616 (Ebuka) - specific Ep 19 examples + POIDH motto close
pick_decision_matrix.if_optimizing_for_thesis_quote: 6645 (Monksage) - only one to name Ep 19 + Kenny in copy
pick_decision_matrix.if_optimizing_for_adoption_cta: 6644 (Akukiwil) - 'post your bounty now at poidh.xyz' converter copy

claude_summary.recommended_winner_pick_logic: Pick depends on what 'best ad' means: (a) ad that BEST DESCRIBES the episode = 6616, (b) ad that PERFORMED best in the wild = 6608, (c) ad that CITES the source = 6645, (d) ad that CONVERTS to action = 6644. Watch all 4 videos. If 6616's video matches its copy quality, it's the winner. Otherwise 6608 for distribution metrics.
claude_summary.rejection_template_for_6634: Round 2 required submitting an X post URL per rule 3. Your submission contained only text, no link to an X post. Encourage to re-submit on future bounties with the video link included.
